name: Transact Package Examples
description: Create runnable transaction examples for the @algorandfoundation/algokit-utils/transact subpackage. IMPORTANT: After implementing each example, you MUST run it to verify it works: `npx tsx <example>/index.ts`. If you encounter LocalNet state issues (e.g., account already exists, insufficient funds), reset LocalNet with: `algokit localnet reset`. Always ensure LocalNet is running before testing: `algokit localnet start`.

userStories.title: Infrastructure Setup - Shared Utilities
userStories.description: As a developer, I need shared utilities and constants for all examples so that examples have consistent LocalNet connection and account management.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/transact/shared/constants.ts with LocalNet config (algod/kmd servers, ports, tokens)

Create examples/transact/shared/utils.ts with: createAlgodClient(), createKmdClient(), getFundedAccount(), createRandomAccount(), waitForConfirmation(), getAccountBalance(), formatAlgo(), printHeader(), printStep(), printInfo(), printSuccess(), shortenAddress()
Create examples/transact/package.json referencing file:../../dist for @algorandfoundation/algokit-utils
Create examples/transact/tsconfig.json with ESM module settings
Create examples/transact/README.md documenting all examples
npm install runs without errors in examples/transact directory
TypeScript compiles without errors: npx tsc --noEmit

userStories.title: Payment Transaction Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to send ALGO between accounts using the transact package.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/transact/01-payment-transaction/index.ts
Demonstrate Transaction class with TransactionType.Payment
Use PaymentTransactionFields with receiver and amount
Use assignFee() to set transaction fee from suggested params
Use generateAddressWithSigners for signing
Submit transaction and wait for confirmation
Verify receiver balance increased by sent amount
Console output follows format: header, numbered steps, indented info, success message
Run the example with `npx tsx 01-payment-transaction/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Payment with Close Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to close an account by transferring all remaining ALGO to another account.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/transact/02-payment-close/index.ts
Demonstrate closeRemainderTo field in PaymentTransactionFields
Fund a temporary account, then close it to another account
Verify closed account has 0 balance after transaction
Verify close-to account received the remainder (original - sent - fee)
Run the example with `npx tsx 02-payment-close/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Asset Create Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to create a new Algorand Standard Asset (ASA) with all configuration fields.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/transact/03-asset-create/index.ts
Demonstrate TransactionType.AssetConfig for asset creation
Set all AssetConfigTransactionFields: total, decimals, defaultFrozen, assetName, unitName, url, manager, reserve, freeze, clawback
Retrieve created asset ID from pending transaction info
Verify asset exists with correct parameters using algod.assetById()
Verify creator holds total supply
Run the example with `npx tsx 03-asset-create/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Asset Transfer Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see the full asset transfer flow: create, opt-in, and transfer.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/transact/04-asset-transfer/index.ts
Create an asset first
Demonstrate opt-in transaction (receiver sends 0 amount to self)
Transfer assets from creator to opted-in receiver
Use AssetTransferTransactionFields with assetId, receiver, amount
Verify receiver's asset balance after transfer
Run the example with `npx tsx 04-asset-transfer/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Asset Freeze Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to freeze and unfreeze asset holdings.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/transact/05-asset-freeze/index.ts
Create an asset with freeze address set
Transfer assets to another account
Demonstrate TransactionType.AssetFreeze to freeze the account
Use AssetFreezeTransactionFields with assetId, freezeTarget, frozen
Verify account is frozen (cannot transfer)
Demonstrate unfreezing the account
Verify account can transfer after unfreeze
Run the example with `npx tsx 05-asset-freeze/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Asset Clawback Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to clawback assets from an account using the clawback address.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/transact/06-asset-clawback/index.ts
Create an asset with clawback address set
Transfer assets to another account
Demonstrate clawback using assetSender field in AssetTransferTransactionFields
Clawback transaction sent by clawback address, takes from target, sends to receiver
Verify target account balance decreased
Verify clawback receiver received the assets
Run the example with `npx tsx 06-asset-clawback/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Atomic Group Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to group multiple transactions atomically.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/transact/07-atomic-group/index.ts
Create 3 payment transactions to different receivers
Use groupTransactions() to assign group ID to all transactions
Sign all transactions with the same signer
Submit as a single group using sendRawTransaction with concatenated bytes
Verify all receivers received their amounts
Demonstrate that group transactions succeed or fail together
Run the example with `npx tsx 07-atomic-group/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Atomic Swap Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to perform an atomic swap of ALGO for ASA between two parties.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/transact/08-atomic-swap/index.ts
Create an asset owned by party A
Party B opts into the asset
Build atomic group: Party A sends ASA to B, Party B sends ALGO to A
Use groupTransactions() to create atomic group
Each party signs only their transaction in the group
Combine signatures and submit
Verify A received ALGO and B received ASA
Run the example with `npx tsx 08-atomic-swap/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Single Signature Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to create a keypair and sign transactions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/transact/09-single-sig/index.ts
Demonstrate creating a keypair using tweetnacl
Use generateAddressWithSigners() to create AddressWithSigners from keypair
Show the ed25519 public key and derived Algorand address
Create and sign a payment transaction
Explain the signing process in comments
Run the example with `npx tsx 09-single-sig/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Multisig Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to create and use a 2-of-3 multisig account.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/transact/10-multisig/index.ts
Create 3 individual accounts
Create MultisigAccount with version=1, threshold=2, and all 3 addresses
Show the derived multisig address
Fund the multisig address
Create a payment transaction from the multisig
Sign with 2 of the 3 accounts using MultisigAccount.signer
Submit and verify the transaction succeeds
Demonstrate that 1 signature is insufficient
Run the example with `npx tsx 10-multisig/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Logic Signature Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to use a logic signature to authorize transactions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/transact/11-logic-sig/index.ts
Compile a simple TEAL program using algod.tealCompile()
Create LogicSig from the compiled program bytes
Show the logic signature address
Fund the logic signature address
Create LogicSigAccount and use its signer to authorize a payment
Submit transaction authorized by the logic signature
Demonstrate delegated logic signature (account delegates to lsig)
Run the example with `npx tsx 11-logic-sig/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Fee Calculation Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to estimate transaction size and calculate fees.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/transact/12-fee-calculation/index.ts
Create various transaction types (payment, asset transfer, app call)
Use estimateTransactionSize() to get estimated byte size
Use calculateFee() with different fee parameters
Demonstrate assignFee() to set fee on transaction
Show how feePerByte, minFee, extraFee, and maxFee work
Compare estimated vs actual transaction sizes
Run the example with `npx tsx 12-fee-calculation/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Encoding/Decoding Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to serialize and deserialize transactions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/transact/13-encoding-decoding/index.ts
Create a transaction object
Use encodeTransaction() to get msgpack bytes with TX prefix
Use encodeTransactionRaw() to get msgpack bytes without prefix
Use decodeTransaction() to reconstruct transaction from bytes
Verify decoded transaction matches original
Demonstrate encodeSignedTransaction() and decodeSignedTransaction()
Show transaction ID calculation using txId()
Run the example with `npx tsx 13-encoding-decoding/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Application Call Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to deploy and call a smart contract.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/transact/14-app-call/index.ts
Compile simple approval and clear TEAL programs
Create app with TransactionType.AppCall and OnApplicationComplete.NoOp
Use AppCallTransactionFields with approvalProgram, clearStateProgram, globalStateSchema, localStateSchema
Retrieve created app ID from pending transaction info
Call the app with application arguments
Demonstrate OnApplicationComplete.OptIn for local state
Delete the app at the end
Run the example with `npx tsx 14-app-call/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Verification Script
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a script to run all examples and verify they work.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/transact/verify-all.sh bash script
Script runs each example in order (01 through 14)
Script reports success/failure for each example
Script exits with non-zero code if any example fails
Add npm script 'verify-all' to package.json
All 14 examples pass when running ./verify-all.sh
